todaysEvents.summary: Iran activated the 'Tehran Toll Booth' on Mar 27 - IRGC-controlled corridor between Qeshm and Larak islands charging ~$2M per vessel transit payable in Chinese yuan; at least 2 vessels confirmed paid; Iranian parliament drafting bill to legislate the toll (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, NBC News, Fortune). (Mar 27)
todaysEvents.delta: Iran activates Hormuz toll - $2M/vessel in yuan

todaysEvents.summary: Trump extended the pause on Iranian power plant strikes to April 6 via Truth Social post on Mar 26-27; Iran FM Araghchi denied direct negotiations: 'no negotiations have happened, and we do not plan on any'; Pakistan continues relaying messages as intermediary (Euronews, NPR, Al Jazeera). (Mar 27)
todaysEvents.delta: Trump extends deadline to Apr 6; Iran denies talks

todaysEvents.summary: Brent crude closed at $112.57/bbl on Mar 27 - highest level since July 2022 per CNBC; WTI briefly crossed $100/bbl intraday (session high $100.04, FX Leaders); iTraxx Asia IG est. ~178bp (+6bp); toll booth activation added a de-dollarization signal as Iran collects yuan-only fees. (Mar 27)

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Pakistan-hosted talks yield an interim framework: toll booth suspended, Iran accepts partial sovereignty language, Trump refrains from power plant strikes through April 6. A 14-day ceasefire window opens. Brent retreats to $90-100/bbl; JKM pulls back to $17-19 on resumed Qatar damage assessment. P&I cover only partially restored - toll booth precedent means full commercial Hormuz passage requires a separate legal framework. Israel's stated independence from US-Iran talks remains the key wildcard.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Tehran Toll Booth (activated Mar 27 at $2M/vessel in yuan) becomes the new normal: some tankers pay to transit, others reroute via Cape. Iran's sovereignty precedent hardens. Pakistan-hosted talks begin but move slowly; Trump extends April 6 deadline again. Hormuz functions at reduced, tolled capacity - commercial P&I cover not restored. Brent settles $110-130/bbl; JKM at $19-23/MMBtu. Iran parliament legislates the toll, entrenching yuan-denominated petrotrade. Israel-Hezbollah Lebanon front sustains. iTraxx Asia IG pushes through 185bp.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Trump resumes power plant strikes after April 6 upon Iran's toll booth legislation passing parliament. Iran revokes non-hostile framework, closes toll corridor; full commercial blockade reinstated. IRGC resumes full offensive operations against US/Saudi targets; Strait of Malacca closure threat reactivated. Ras Laffan offline for the quarter. Oil hits $160-190/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk surges - Thailand and Philippines face double-digit fuel inflation and sovereign credit-rating action. De-dollarization cascade accelerates as yuan-settled energy trade expands beyond Hormuz.
